trans-unit.source: __Layers List / Browser Panel:__ The __layers list__ shows __all layers/files__ that are __loaded in the project__. You can show/hide layers and set other properties.
trans-unit.target: __Layers List / Browser Panel:__ The __layers list__ shows __all layers/files__ that are __loaded in the project__. You can show/hide layers and set other properties.

trans-unit.source: __Toolsbars: __Toolbars__ are shortcuts__ to execute frequently used commands. For example, there are special toolbars for __vector and raster files__, but also general ones for saving your project, etc. The toolbar contains, among other things, a list of all the commands you can use. The toolbar also contains the __toolbox__, which is used later in many of the wiki videos.
trans-unit.target: __Toolsbars: __Toolbars__ are shortcuts__ to execute frequently used commands. For example, there are special toolbars for __vector and raster files__, but also general ones for saving your project, etc. The toolbar contains, among other things, a list of all the commands you can use. The toolbar also contains the __toolbox__, which is used later in many of the wiki videos.

trans-unit.source: __Map View:__ The __map view__ is the __central component__ of every GIS programme. This is where the __geodata__ is displayed. The map view has a projection which does not always have to correspond to the projection of the layers.
trans-unit.target: __Map View:__ The __map view__ is the __central component__ of every GIS programme. This is where the __geodata__ is displayed. The map view has a projection which does not always have to correspond to the projection of the layers.

trans-unit.source: __Status bar:__ In the __status bar__ you will find __central information about the current map view__. Here you can set the __projection of the map view and the scale__. You can read the coordinates of the mouse pointer and thus quickly find out the coordinates of points on the map. You can rotate your map view, e.g. if you want to create a map facing south.
trans-unit.target: __Status bar:__ In the __status bar__ you will find __central information about the current map view__. Here you can set the __projection of the map view and the scale__. You can read the coordinates of the mouse pointer and thus quickly find out the coordinates of points on the map. You can rotate your map view, e.g. if you want to create a map facing south.

trans-unit.source: __Side Toolbar__. You may see a __side toolbar__. This is another way to easily open vector and raster files in QGIS.
trans-unit.target: __Side Toolbar__. You may see a __side toolbar__. This is another way to easily open vector and raster files in QGIS.

trans-unit.source: __Locator bar__. Here you can __search for tools and layers__. If you don't know where to find a tool, you can try here.
trans-unit.target: __Locator bar__. Here you can __search for tools and layers__. If you don't know where to find a tool, you can try here.

trans-unit.source: Navigation in the map view
trans-unit.target: Navigation in the map view

trans-unit.source: Pans the map to the selected element
trans-unit.target: Pans the map to the selected element

trans-unit.source: Opens the Dialog to create a new print layout
trans-unit.target: Opens the Dialog to create a new print layout

trans-unit.source: Identify features on the map view by clicking on them
trans-unit.target: Identify features on the map view by clicking on them

trans-unit.source: Select a feature by area or single click
trans-unit.target: Select a feature by area or single click

trans-unit.source: Open attribute table with selected features only
trans-unit.target: Open attribute table with selected features only

trans-unit.source: Open the attribute table with selected features only
trans-unit.target: Open the attribute table with selected features only

trans-unit.source: Open attribute table with visible features only
trans-unit.target: Open attribute table with visible features only

trans-unit.source: Open the attribute table with visible features only
trans-unit.target: Open the attribute table with visible features only

trans-unit.source: Moving an orientation on the Map Canvas
trans-unit.target: Moving an orientation on the Map Canvas

trans-unit.source: To move on the map canvas with your mouse cursor you need to toggle the hand button.
trans-unit.target: To move on the map canvas with your mouse cursor you need to toggle the hand button.

trans-unit.source: You can also move on the map canvas with arrow keys on your keyboard.
trans-unit.target: You can also move on the map canvas with arrow keys on your keyboard.

trans-unit.source: Zooming in the map view
trans-unit.target: Zooming in the map view

trans-unit.source: The easiest way to zoom on Map Canvas is by __scrolling__.
trans-unit.target: The easiest way to zoom on Map Canvas is by __scrolling__.

trans-unit.source: Another way is to use the zoom buttons in the toolbox panel.
trans-unit.target: Another way is to use the zoom buttons in the toolbox panel.

trans-unit.source: All the functionality, tools and applications of QGIS are organised in the __Processing toolbox__. If you ever need to find a tool, or an algorithm, you can open the toolbox and use the search function to find it.
trans-unit.target: All the functionality, tools and applications of QGIS are organised in the __Processing toolbox__. If you ever need to find a tool, or an algorithm, you can open the toolbox and use the search function to find it.

trans-unit.source: To open the Toolbox in QGIS click on the gearwheel button. Or click on `Processing` → `Toolbox`.
trans-unit.target: To open the Toolbox in QGIS click on the gearwheel button. Or click on `Processing` → `Toolbox`.

trans-unit.source: You can use the search bar to find specific tools.
trans-unit.target: You can use the search bar to find specific tools.

trans-unit.source: Some Tools have their own toolbars which you can add to your QGIS interface. They appear above the map canvas as icons which let you quickly access specific functionalities. To avoid an overcrowded interface it is smart to only activate specific toolbars or panels only when you really need them.
trans-unit.target: Some Tools have their own toolbars which you can add to your QGIS interface. They appear above the map canvas as icons which let you quickly access specific functionalities. To avoid an overcrowded interface it is smart to only activate specific toolbars or panels only when you really need them.

trans-unit.source: Show and hide displays and toolbars
trans-unit.target: Show and hide displays and toolbars

trans-unit.source: To add or remove toolbars from your interface click on `View` → `Toolbars` → Check or uncheck the toolboxes you want to add or remove.
trans-unit.target: To add or remove toolbars from your interface click on `View` → `Toolbars` → Check or uncheck the toolboxes you want to add or remove.

trans-unit.source: To add or remove panels from your interface click on `View` → `Panels` → Check or uncheck the panels you want to add or remove.
trans-unit.target: To add or remove panels from your interface click on `View` → `Panels` → Check or uncheck the panels you want to add or remove.

trans-unit.source: At each toolbar there is a field of two dotted lines. If you move the mouse pointer over it until an arrow cross appears and then hold down the left mouse button, you can move the toolbar. This allows an individualised arrangement of your own tools. By compressing all toolbars into a few lines, the map view window can also be enlarged.
trans-unit.target: At each toolbar there is a field of two dotted lines. If you move the mouse pointer over it until an arrow cross appears and then hold down the left mouse button, you can move the toolbar. This allows an individualised arrangement of your own tools. By compressing all toolbars into a few lines, the map view window can also be enlarged.

trans-unit.source: To save progress or to open an existing project in QGIS is very similar to programs like MS Word. However, there is one __BIG__ difference. In QGIS the geodata you work with is __not__ saved in your QGIS project file. Instead, the project file only contains the file paths where the geodata were located at the time the project was last saved on the PC. If the location of this geodata is subsequently changed, the error message "handle unavailable layers" will appear when the project is opened again.
trans-unit.target: To save progress or to open an existing project in QGIS is very similar to programs like MS Word. However, there is one __BIG__ difference. In QGIS the geodata you work with is __not__ saved in your QGIS project file. Instead, the project file only contains the file paths where the geodata were located at the time the project was last saved on the PC. If the location of this geodata is subsequently changed, the error message "handle unavailable layers" will appear when the project is opened again.

trans-unit.source: To open an existing QGIS project click on `Project` → `Open…` → Navigate to your project and open it.
trans-unit.target: To open an existing QGIS project click on `Project` → `Open…` → Navigate to your project and open it.

trans-unit.source: __When you save the first time__: To save the QGIS project you are working on click on `Project` → `Save as…` → Navigate to the folder where you want to save the project → Give the project a name → `Save`.
trans-unit.target: __When you save the first time__: To save the QGIS project you are working on click on `Project` → `Save as…` → Navigate to the folder where you want to save the project → Give the project a name → `Save`.

trans-unit.source: __When you save progress__: To save progress in a project that was already saved somewhere on your computer there are two good options:
trans-unit.target: __When you save progress__: To save progress in a project that was already saved somewhere on your computer there are two good options:

trans-unit.source: Use the hotkey <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>S</kbd> on your keyboard.
trans-unit.target: Use the hotkey <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>S</kbd> on your keyboard.
